# Vision HF differential goldens (ADR-069)

HF `transformers` reference output for the Qwen3.5-0.8B vision path, pinned so future engine
stages (S3-S5 of ADR-069) have a committed cross-framework target instead of building blind.
This directory contains fixture data only — no lattice engine code reads it yet.

Regenerate with:

```bash
uv run python scripts/vision_goldens_qwen35.py \
    --model-dir ~/.lattice/models/qwen3.5-0.8b \
    --out tests/fixtures/vision/
```

## Pinned methodology

- **Checkpoint**: `~/.lattice/models/qwen3.5-0.8b`. Its `config.json` and
  `model.safetensors.index.json` are verified byte-identical to HF Hub
  `Qwen/Qwen3.5-0.8B` commit `2fc06364715b967f1860aea9cf38778875588b17` at generation time
  (the script re-checks this every run and fails closed on drift).
- **Runtime**: `transformers==5.12.1`, `torch==2.13.0`, `torchvision==0.28.0`, `pillow==12.3.0`,
  `numpy==2.4.6`, Python 3.11.12, CPU, float32, single BLAS thread
  (`torch.set_num_threads(1)`). All six are pinned exactly and validated before any output is
  created or the checkpoint is touched (`validate_runtime_versions`/`preflight` in the script);
  numpy is included because it drives the deterministic procedural PNG. Exact versions are
  re-recorded in `manifest.json["source"]` on every regeneration — trust that field over this
  prose if they disagree.
- **Image**: `golden_image.png`, 256x256, generated in-script from a fixed seed (`20260714`) —
  an RGB gradient background plus four solid shapes with a small seeded coordinate jitter. Fully
  deterministic; no camera/network image is used.
- **Prompt**: `"Describe this image."` rendered through the checkpoint's chat template with
  `add_generation_prompt=True` (default non-thinking mode — see Findings below).
- **Decode**: greedy (`do_sample=False`), 8 new tokens, `pad_token_id` pinned to the tokenizer's
  EOS id.

## Fixtures

| File | Consumed by | Contents |
|---|---|---|
| `golden_image.png` | S3 | The fixed 256x256 procedural test image. |
| `manifest.json` | all stages | Central index: source/version provenance, image spec, decode params, `image_grid_thw`, tensor shapes/dtypes/sha256 for every fixture file, and the sanity-check results below. |
| `pixel_values_summary` (in `manifest.json`) | S3 | Shape + mean/std/min/max of the processor's `pixel_values` tensor (not the full tensor — see Fixture format). |
| `vit_pre_merger_f32.bin` | **S3** | Full-precision ViT pre-merger patch features, `last_hidden_state` from `Qwen3_5VisionModel.forward`, shape `(256, 768)` = `(num_patches, vision hidden_size)`. S3's gate is cosine > 0.999 against this tensor. |
| `vit_post_merger_f32.bin` | **S3** (secondary) / S4/S5 input shape | Full-precision post-merger projected visual embeddings, `pooler_output`, shape `(64, 1024)` = `(num_visual_tokens, out_hidden_size)` — the exact tensor injected into the decoder's residual stream. |
| `input_ids.json` | **S4** | The full input token id stream after image-placeholder expansion (82 tokens) — the exact ids the decoder sees, including the 64 expanded `<|image_pad|>` (id `248056`) tokens. |
| `position_ids.json` | **S5** | The 3-axis (`t`, `h`, `w`) position ids from `Qwen3_5Model.get_rope_index` for this exact sequence, shape `(3, 82)`. |
| `greedy_tokens.json` | **S5** | The first 8 greedy tokens generated by the full HF model on this image + prompt — the future S5 end-to-end parity target. |

## Fixture format

JSON for scalars/ids/shapes/summaries; raw little-endian `float32` `.bin` for the two full
embedding tensors (`vit_pre_merger_f32.bin`, `vit_post_merger_f32.bin`), row-major, shape recorded
in `manifest.json`. Total committed size is ~1.0 MB (well under the ~5 MB budget) — the two `.bin`
files are ~768 KB and ~256 KB respectively; nothing needed truncating.

## Determinism proof

Ran the generator twice into separate output directories on the same machine/checkpoint/pinned
versions:

```
$ diff -rq tests/fixtures/vision/ <second-run-dir>/
(no output — files identical)
$ shasum -a 256 * | sort   # both runs
(identical sha256 for every file, including the two .bin tensors and golden_image.png)
```

## Sanity checks (from `manifest.json["sanity_checks"]`, asserted by the generator — a non-zero
exit means one failed)

- `post_merger_count_matches_placeholder_count`: **true** — 64 post-merger visual embeddings ==
  64 `<|image_pad|>` tokens in `input_ids.json`.
- `text_prefix_positions_collapse_1d`: **true** — for every position before the first
  `<|vision_start|>` token, `t == h == w` in `position_ids.json` (positions `0..4`: `[0,1,2,3]` on
  all three axes).
- `image_span_positions_diverge`: **true** — across the 64 image-token positions, the `h` and `w`
  axes differ (e.g. `t` stays `4` for the whole image span while `h`/`w` sweep the 8x8 merged
  grid), then the three axes reconverge at position `70` (`[16,17,18,...]` on all axes) for the
  trailing text.

## Findings (HF-side surprises worth recording)

- **The checkpoint directory does not ship a processor.** `~/.lattice/models/qwen3.5-0.8b` has
  `config.json`, `model.safetensors*`, and `tokenizer.json` only — no `tokenizer_config.json`,
  `chat_template.jinja`, `preprocessor_config.json`, or `video_preprocessor_config.json`. The
  script fetches those four small text files from the pinned HF Hub commit after verifying
  `config.json` matches byte-for-byte, and merges them with the local weights via symlinks in a
  scratch directory. Model weights are never fetched from the network.
- **The processor's own `min_pixels` floor is 65536 (256x256), not the Qwen2VL library default of
  `56*56`.** `preprocessor_config.json` overrides `size.shortest_edge` to `65536`. An image
  smaller than 256x256 gets upscaled by `smart_resize`, so 256x256 is the actual smallest
  "no-upscale" grid, not a library default — this is why the fixture image is 256x256 rather than
  something smaller.
- **`Qwen3VLProcessor` (used for Qwen3.5, not a Qwen3.5-specific processor class) requires a
  `video_processor` instance, not `None`**, even for an image-only, no-video call — its
  `ProcessorMixin.__init__` type-checks the argument. The Qwen3.5 video processor
  (`Qwen3VLVideoProcessor`) in turn requires `torchvision` to import, even though this fixture
  never processes video. `torchvision` is now a pinned dev-dependency for exactly this reason.
- **The chat template auto-inserts an empty thinking block.** With
  `add_generation_prompt=True`, the rendered prompt ends in `<think>\n\n</think>\n\n` even though
  no thinking content was requested — Qwen3.5's default is non-thinking mode expressed as an
  empty think block, not the absence of one. `input_ids.json` reflects this; anything that
  reconstructs the prompt string for comparison must include it.
- **`get_image_features` splits `pooler_output` in HF's own multi-image API**, but for the
  single-image case used here the full `(64, 1024)` tensor from `Qwen3_5VisionModel.forward`
  directly is what `vit_post_merger_f32.bin` holds — no windowing/splitting was needed for a
  single 256x256 image with `image_grid_thw = [[1, 16, 16]]`.
